{{Short description="Rate of change of position; speed with direction}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = Velocity
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Kinematics]], [[Motion]], [[Speed]], [[Acceleration]], [[Momentum]]
| symbol = v
| SI_unit = [[metre per second]] (m/s)
| type = [[Vector (physics)|Vector]]
}}

'''Velocity''' is the rate of change of the [[position (geometry)|position]] of an object with respect to a frame of reference, and is a function of [[time]]. Velocity is a [[vector (physics)|vector]] quantity — it has both magnitude ([[speed]]) and direction. The [[SI unit]] is [[metre per second]] (m/s).

:<math>\mathbf{v} = \frac{d\mathbf{r}}{dt}</math>

where <math>\mathbf{r}</math> is the position vector.

== Distinction from speed ==
* '''Velocity''' (<math>\mathbf{v}</math>) — vector: describes both how fast and in what direction
* '''Speed''' (<math>v = |\mathbf{v}|</math>) — scalar: magnitude of velocity, directionless

An object moving in a circle at constant speed has a constantly changing velocity (direction changes).

== Equations of motion (constant acceleration) ==
:<math>v = v_0 + at</math>
:<math>v^2 = v_0^2 + 2a\Delta x</math>
:<math>\Delta x = \frac{v_0 + v}{2}t</math>

== Relative velocity ==
Velocities in different reference frames add vectorially (Galilean transformation for <math>v \ll c</math>):
:<math>\mathbf{v}_{AB} = \mathbf{v}_{AC} + \mathbf{v}_{CB}</math>

For [[special relativity]], use relativistic velocity addition:
:<math>u' = \frac{u - v}{1 - uv/c^2}</math>

== Terminal velocity ==
When [[drag (physics)|drag force]] balances [[gravity]], an object falls at constant velocity:
:<math>v_t = \sqrt{\frac{2mg}{\rho A C_d}}</math>

== Velocity in momentum and kinetic energy ==
* [[Momentum]]: <math>\mathbf{p} = m\mathbf{v}</math>
* [[Kinetic energy]]: <math>E_k = \frac{1}{2}mv^2</math>

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Acceleration]]
* [[Kinematics]]
* [[Momentum]]
* [[Motion]]
* [[Speed]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:Velocity| ]]
[[Category:Kinematics]]
[[Category:Motion (physics)]]
[[Category:Physical quantities]]
